channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: No pedestrian access, 17th March
item.description:
The crossing roadworks on the corner of Homesdale Road and Mornington Avenue have no provisions for pedestrian access. I cannot get from my house to my car with my sons wheelchair. I appreciate this is temporary, however I need to leave the house. Surely a provision should be made when the path is closed?
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Utility Works and Temporary Traffic Lights

item.title: Dumped Untaxed car, 17th March
item.description:
Hello this car has been dumped here with no TAX for 3 to 4 months , never moved. My mother is disabled and this is stopping her nurses from parking outside as parking is a struggle

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): North Cray Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Test (ignore) - FAO Anjna, 17th March
item.description:
Testing if default address is pulled through

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Roseneath Walk, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car parked wrong way in one-way street with number plates removed, 17th March
item.description:
Black BMW registration X33RKS has been parked outside number 16 Perry Hall Road, facing the wrong way, since the end of January. I spoke to two passing police officers on February 9th but they weren't interested because they "didn't see the offence being committed". As we were talking, two young men from KwikFit came over and said they'd pushed the car there because it had broken down. As it had valid tax and MoT the police accepted this and just told them to turn it round when they could. More than a month later it's still facing the wrong way, and now the number plates have been removed, which seems suspicious to me. What are they trying to hide? And why aren't the police doing anything? Parking in this section of Perry Hall Road is difficult at the best of times. I believe this car should have been parked on KwikFit's forecourt but suspect it belongs to staff rather than a customer and therefore isn't allowed. I have reported two other vehicles in the immediate vicinity - one has been declared SORN and the other has no MoT. Residents are fed up with cars being dumped here.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Hall Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tip, 17th March
item.description:
Broken bed frame dumped

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hartville Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Uplifted tree roots causing serious pavement defect, 17th March
item.description:
Dangerous pavement defect caused by uplifted tree roots on this strip of the street. The surface is uneven and poses a serious tripping hazard, particularly for pedestrians, children, and elderly residents. Given the risk of falls and potential injury, I request urgent inspection and repair to make the area safe as soon as possible.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 75, Burnt Ash Lane, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Coffee table flytipped, 17th March
item.description:
Small white table dumped near the railway arch and opposite dry cleaners on Parish Lane

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bycroft Street, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffitti on communal wall by 11 Grove Close and 6 to 7 Grove Close SE23 1AS, 17th March
item.description:
Graffitti on communal wall by 11 Grove Close and 6 to 7 Grove Close SE23 1AS

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Malham Road, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car with expired MoT apparently abandoned, 17th March
item.description:
Silver Audi A1 registration FW12BYB has been parked outside number 22 Perry Hall Road for months and would appear to have been abandoned. Its MoT expired at the beginning of February. Parking is difficult in this stretch of road and it appears to be becoming a dumping ground for unlawful vehicles.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Hall Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No street Cleaning, 17th March
item.description:
Extension of Lynmouth Rd to the junction with Osbaldeston Rd is not being regularly swept. Repeatedly reported here. It gets a sweep but is not maintained. Please sort out the rotas to include this busy street used by many children throughout the day.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Osbaldeston Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: All round fishing lake, 17th March
item.description:
The swims on the fishing lake very unsafe some of them falling apart no maintenance at all and a lot of letter in the bushes around the lake please be looked into

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cobourg Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road Surface is Terrible, 17th March
item.description:
The road surface here is awful; its like driving through acne. Its bumpy, full of holes, uneven, and its dangerous for motorcycles and cycles i think that whole stretch needs resurfacing. Thank you
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crown Lane, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car declared SORN parked in public road, 17th March
item.description:
Black BMW registration YE66OAB has been declared SORN but is parked in a public road - outside number 14 Perry Hall Road - and is taking up a valuable parking space. This stretch of road appears to be becoming a dumping ground for unlawful vehicles.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Hall Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road sign 'Broad Oaks Way', 17th March
item.description:
Road sign in front of 54 Broad Oaks Way was damaged by a vehicle colliding with it in January 2026. A brick pier at entrance to our drive was also damaged and will be replaced by the insurers. They suggest that the road sign should be replaced/repaired after the brickwork is completed. Please let me know how to inform you once this has been done.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 52, Broad Oaks Way, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge potholes on this road, 17th March
item.description:
I drive up and down this road regularly start of the year.. It's fast becoming a shortcut to avoid the main woolwich road works. There are at least 4 potholes wider than 30cm. They're very dangerous.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): St Mary Street, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Temporary traffic lights have been moved directly in front of the building entrance at 10 Portman Square, London W1H 6AZ, which is currently obstructing access to the entrance., 17th March
item.description:
Temporary traffic lights have been moved directly in front of the building entrance at 10 Portman Square, London W1H 6AZ, which is currently obstructing access to the entrance.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 12-14, Portman Square, Soho, City of Westminster
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned outside 105 not 108 Ancona Road, 17th March
item.description:
Smashed back window and no number plates

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ancona Road, Plumstead

item.title: Bag of rubble dumped by tree, 17th March
item.description:
Bag of building rubble dumped by foot of tree, it has been here a few days

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Southborough Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vehicle abandoned, 17th March
item.description:
This red van arrived on Saturday and has since had the wheels removed/stolen It is taking up valuable parking from residents and doesn’t appear to have an owner nearby

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cattistock Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Building rubbish in alleyway, 17th March
item.description:
I have reported this before, and the people who came out looked in the two wrong alleyways! This is located in the alleyway beside 42 Granby Road leading to Arsenal Rd

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Granby Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet
